coverOverlay.hook: You always look after, never before
coverOverlay.subhook: And you already know why you wait ->

bodySlides.headline: You buy the thing first. You check the number after.
bodySlides.sub: At 1am. Not to plan. To brace.

bodySlides.headline: You look after so the number can't stop you.
bodySlides.sub: It can only grade you.

bodySlides.headline: Before, the number gets a vote. After, it gets a verdict.
bodySlides.sub: You pick the verdict on purpose.

bodySlides.definition: Looking at your money once it is too late to change the answer.

bodySlides.headline: This week, before one buy, look at the number first.
bodySlides.sub: Same app. Same number. This time it gets a say.

endSlide.question: How long since you looked before buying?
endSlide.repLine: Days, weeks, years — drop it. No judgment.

coverOverlay.subhook: Guess the number. Then open the app, once →

bodySlides.definition: The balance you can feel but can't say out loud.

bodySlides.headline: The fog is the fear doing its job.
bodySlides.sub: It keeps you from looking at all.

bodySlides.headline: The gap is your flinch, measured.
bodySlides.sub: From your guess to the real number.

bodySlides.headline: Say your guess. Then open the app, once.
bodySlides.sub: One look. Nothing more.

bodySlides.headline: You never had a math problem.
bodySlides.sub: You had a look-away problem.

endSlide.question: How far off were you? Twenty? A hundred?
endSlide.repLine: Dropping the gap counts as your first rep.

coverOverlay.hook: It survived a week. Then a thought.
coverOverlay.subhook: The real cause of death is on slide 4 →

bodySlides.headline: Your budget makes it eight days. Every time.
bodySlides.sub: Days one through seven, you are fine. Day eight, one slip.

bodySlides.headline: The slip is not what kills it.
bodySlides.sub: One bad day does not end a month. You know this.

bodySlides.definition: The moment after a slip when you decide the month is ruined now.

bodySlides.headline: So you stop looking until the 1st.
bodySlides.sub: Three clean weeks die for one bad day.

bodySlides.headline: After a slip, your month restarts tomorrow morning.
bodySlides.sub: Not the 1st. Tomorrow.

endSlide.question: What day does yours usually die?

coverOverlay.hook: The folder you feed but never open.
coverOverlay.subhook: The pile isn't proof. Swipe to count it →

bodySlides.headline: You built a folder for the fear.
bodySlides.sub: Every bank alert lands there, unread.

bodySlides.headline: The pile keeps growing.
bodySlides.sub: And it starts to feel like proof.

bodySlides.definition: A pile is not a number. It's dread with a badge.

bodySlides.headline: So do one thing. Count them.
bodySlides.sub: Don't open any.

bodySlides.headline: Dread says hundreds.
bodySlides.sub: The real number is smaller than the feeling.

endSlide.question: Count yours tonight. What's the real number?
endSlide.repLine: Counting them counts as your first rep.

coverOverlay.hook: Tonight, you look. You fix nothing.
coverOverlay.subhook: The whole plan fits one swipe →

bodySlides.headline: Tonight, you only look
bodySlides.sub: No moving cash. No math. No new plan.

bodySlides.headline: Your brain braces for a fight
bodySlides.sub: You wait for the number to hurt.

bodySlides.definition: You look, no fight comes, and the fear gets smaller.

bodySlides.headline: Look, breathe, close the app
bodySlides.sub: Next time, the door is lighter.

bodySlides.headline: You never had a math problem
bodySlides.sub: You had a door you stopped opening.

endSlide.question: How many days since your last look?
endSlide.repLine: Opening the app tonight is your first rep.

coverOverlay.hook: The account name that keeps it empty
coverOverlay.subhook: Cause of death is one word long. Swipe.

bodySlides.headline: Your savings account is named "Savings."
bodySlides.sub: That is why it stays empty.

bodySlides.headline: "Savings" is a word with no story.
bodySlides.sub: So pulling money out costs you nothing.

bodySlides.definition: A label so blank your brain feels no loss when you take from it.

bodySlides.headline: Rename it. "March rent buffer." "Leave any job fund."
bodySlides.sub: Same money. Now taking from it means taking March's rent. Your brain feels that.

bodySlides.headline: You never had a math problem.
bodySlides.sub: You had a name problem.

endSlide.question: Still says "Savings"? Drop the new name below.
endSlide.repLine: Naming it counts as your first rep.

coverOverlay.hook: Do Not Disturb, but for your money
coverOverlay.subhook: You didn't mute the bank. You muted the news. Swipe.

bodySlides.headline: You didn't mute your bank. You muted the messenger.
bodySlides.sub: The money keeps moving. You stopped hearing about it.

bodySlides.headline: Your phone buzzes. Your chest gets tight.
bodySlides.sub: So you shut the alerts off and call it self-care.

bodySlides.definition: You silenced the buzz, but the flinch stays. So you check at 1am, braced for bad news.

bodySlides.headline: Turn one alert back on. Deposits only.
bodySlides.sub: Now the only time your bank taps you, it's good news.

bodySlides.headline: You're not doing math. You're teaching your body the buzz is safe.
bodySlides.sub: Same phone. New feeling.

endSlide.question: How long has yours been muted? Weeks? Years?
endSlide.repLine: Drop it below. No judgment. Saving this counts as your first rep.

coverOverlay.hook: The round trip nobody admits to
coverOverlay.subhook: See how it sneaks back by Friday →

bodySlides.headline: You save it Monday. You take it back by Friday.
bodySlides.sub: Same money, home before the weekend.

bodySlides.headline: The money in savings still feels like yours.
bodySlides.sub: So when checking gets thin, you bring it home.

bodySlides.definition: noun. a save timed so badly you pull it back by Friday. The plan holds. The timing is what dies.

bodySlides.headline: Move the transfer to payday morning.
bodySlides.sub: Before the money ever lands in your hands.

bodySlides.headline: Money you never met is money you don't mourn.
bodySlides.sub: You can't reach for what never arrived.

endSlide.question: What day does yours crawl back?
endSlide.repLine: Naming the day is your first rep.

coverOverlay.hook: The cart is faster than the doubt
coverOverlay.subhook: One card is why the wanting always wins →

bodySlides.headline: There's one site that gets you at midnight
bodySlides.sub: You know which one.

bodySlides.headline: It's not the wanting that costs you. It's the speed
bodySlides.sub: The want is fine. The two taps are the problem.

bodySlides.definition: Your saved card makes the buy end before the wanting can pass.

bodySlides.headline: Open the site. Settings. Payment. Delete the saved card
bodySlides.sub: You can still buy. It takes ninety more seconds now.

bodySlides.headline: Most midnight wants don't live for ninety seconds
bodySlides.sub: Give the want time and it walks itself out.

endSlide.question: Which site is it? First letter only.
endSlide.repLine: Deleting that card counts as your first rep.

cards.title: We miss you
cards.body: You have not logged a purchase in 34 days.

coverOverlay.subhook: Cause of death, swipe to read it →

bodySlides.headline: You stopped showing up
bodySlides.sub: Not because you are lazy.

bodySlides.headline: Your budget was a job
bodySlides.sub: You hired yourself, and forgot to pay.

bodySlides.definition: Twenty-three categories. One for coffee. One for gas. Night shifts, no days off.

bodySlides.headline: Try three numbers
bodySlides.sub: Bills. Life. Keep.

bodySlides.headline: Small enough to hold in your head
bodySlides.sub: Right there in the checkout line.

endSlide.question: How many categories did yours have?
endSlide.repLine: Drop the number. No judgment counts as a rep.

cards.title: Payment due
cards.body: Your bill is due in 2 days.

coverOverlay.hook: The mid-month jump scare is optional.
coverOverlay.subhook: The 2-minute fix is one swipe away →

bodySlides.definition: A bill that lands before payday, then acts like it is your fault.

bodySlides.headline: No one tells you this: due dates move.
bodySlides.sub: You treat the date like it is set in stone.

bodySlides.headline: Line the due date up with payday.
bodySlides.sub: Same bill. Same amount. Now it lands when the money does.

bodySlides.headline: One bill. Find "change due date."
bodySlides.sub: Pick the day after payday. Two minutes, done.

bodySlides.headline: You don't need more money.
bodySlides.sub: You need a calendar that is on your side.

endSlide.question: What day does yours hit? Drop the number.
endSlide.repLine: Changing one date counts as your first rep.

coverOverlay.hook: You pay to stay someone you're not
coverOverlay.subhook: One tap ends it. Swipe for the rep →

bodySlides.headline: You keep paying for the gym you never OPEN
bodySlides.sub: Same for the course. Same for the app.

bodySlides.headline: You don't pay for the gym. You pay for the STORY
bodySlides.sub: The story that you might go someday.

bodySlides.definition: A charge you leave on. Canceling feels like giving up on who you meant to be.

bodySlides.headline: Open your phone. Find the one you keep out of guilt. Hit CANCEL
bodySlides.sub: Want it back someday? Two taps.

bodySlides.headline: That flinch is not the money. It's the person you were going to be, LEAVING
bodySlides.sub: Let them go.

endSlide.question: How many months have you paid without opening it?
endSlide.repLine: Drop the number. No judgment. That's your rep.
